message: fuzz: cover umount in mount harness

files.patch:
@@ -1340,6 +1340,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-xkc6","title":"POSIX §7.3 conformance table for ctype classification/conversion","description":"ctype::mod has good proptest invariants (prop_core_classification, case_conversion_roundtrip, to_ascii_masks) but no spec-derived table citing POSIX.1-2017 §7.3.1. Add Pattern-4 conformance table with explicit clause refs for is_alpha/is_digit/is_space/is_xdigit/is_cntrl/is_print/is_punct/is_blank and the to_upper/to_lower mapping rules. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ctype/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:40:33.338358031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.032693512Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.030520049Z","close_reason":"Added 2 POSIX §7.3 conformance tables in commit 3d7aa71: classification (10 predicates × in/out-of-class sweeps) + case-mapping (full 256-byte sweep asserting alpha mapping + identity on non-alpha). All cases cite IEEE 1003.1-2017 §7.3.1 or §7.3.2 clauses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xl4sb","title":"PORT: freezero + recallocarray (OpenBSD secret-zeroing allocators)","description":"OpenBSD-origin allocator primitives missing from frankenlibc-abi: freezero(ptr, size) (zero size bytes then free — used for secret-bearing buffers to defeat use-after-free leaks of credentials) and recallocarray(ptr, oldnmemb, nmemb, size) (realloc with overflow check + zero newly-added bytes on grow, and zero released bytes on shrink). Both extend our existing reallocarray to cover the OpenBSD security-hardened allocator API now adopted by glibc 2.34+. Ship as thin shims in malloc_abi.rs delegating to existing realloc/free + memset; no new core module needed since the work is pointer-arithmetic + libc-malloc orchestration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T14:50:29.517540340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T14:53:57.257027350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T14:53:57.255131170Z","close_reason":"shipped: freezero + recallocarray in stdlib_abi.rs delegating to malloc_abi+explicit_bzero; 9 integration tests; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xn6p8","title":"FUZZ: mount/umount/umount2 + fs* family structure-aware harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mount.rs. Targets mount/umount/umount2 and the new mount API (fsopen/fsmount/fsconfig/fspick/open_tree/move_mount/mount_setattr). Covers null-pointer, fuzz-fsname, dirfd variation. Argument validation surface fully exercised independent of root privilege.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-24T22:50:00Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:55:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:55:00Z","close_reason":"Verified fuzz_mount.rs covers mount/umount2/fsopen/fsmount/fsconfig/fspick/open_tree/move_mount/mount_setattr. 11.73M runs at 97K exec/s over 120s, no crashes. Pre-shipped harness verified end-to-end with rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_cc_xn6p8).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-xn6p8.1","title":"FUZZ: add umount coverage to mount syscall harness","description":"Follow-up to bd-xn6p8 discovered by testing-fuzzing review: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mount.rs covers mount, umount2, and the new mount API, but the original bead title/description also promised umount. Add structure-aware umount(target) coverage using the existing safe null/empty/missing target strategy, keep execution privilege-independent, and verify the fuzz target builds through rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-libc","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:40:57Z","created_by":"cod-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:45:25.966351066Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:45:25.963681740Z","close_reason":"Added structure-aware umount coverage to fuzz_mount and verified fuzz target build through rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","mount","syscall"]}
 {"id":"bd-xniy3","title":"PORT: __accept / __bind / __listen / __sendto / __recvfrom / __getsockname / __getpeername (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the seven glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the socket entry points: __accept, __bind, __listen, __sendto, __recvfrom, __getsockname, __getpeername. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols some statically-linked NSS / netdb / inet helpers link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public names in socket_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior as its public counterpart on a fresh AF_UNIX socket pair / loopback connection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:07.523014494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:03:23.669383902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:03:23.667390397Z","close_reason":"shipped: 7 glibc reserved-namespace socket aliases (__accept/__bind/__listen/__sendto/__recvfrom/__getsockname/__getpeername) delegating to existing public-name impls + 2 abi tests covering socketpair round-trip and AF_UNIX listener, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xoqs","title":"POSIX services(5) conformance table for parse_services_line","description":"parse_services_line in frankenlibc_core::resolv has smoke-fuzz coverage (bd-s170) and unit tests but no spec-annotated conformance table. Add Pattern-4 conformance table citing services(5) man page clauses — service-name format, port/protocol grammar, aliases, comments. Complements the existing hosts(5) + resolver(5) conformance suite (bd-x7jd).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:24:38.654768153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.989824462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.987850063Z","close_reason":"Added 12-case services(5) conformance table in commit 73bfe8f. Closes the NSS files-backend parser conformance triad: hosts(5) + resolver(5) + services(5), all with spec_ref citations. Complements bd-s170 smoke-fuzz coverage by pinning spec provenance for each documented grammar rule.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xpgak","title":"xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs","description":"## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.","notes":"Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
